candidate_summary: Feedback-attenuation mapping from forest mast-year synchronization (Moran effect + mycorrhizal signaling + predator satiation) onto distributed LLM training: replicas accumulate in lean years and pulse-broadcast aggregated deltas in mast years, attenuating single-replica byzantine adversarial signals via lean-year accumulation.

results.title: Geography of masting creates greater synchrony in seed scarcity than abundance (PNAS)
results.judge_reason: Directly supports the source-domain mechanism: spatial synchrony of masting, Moran effect, and predator-satiation hypothesis underpinning the analogy.

results.title: Contrasting Resource Dynamics in Mast Years for Beech and Oak (Frontiers)
results.judge_reason: Continental beech/oak mast-year resource dynamics analysis substantiates the lean-year/mast-year reserve dynamics central to the candidate's source mechanism.

results.title: Climate Change Strengthens Selection for Mast Seeding in European Beech (Curr Biol)
results.judge_reason: Confirms satiation-defense logic and climate-driven shifts in mast synchrony, directly relevant to the candidate's source-side mechanism.

results.title: DreamDDP: Layer-wise Scheduled Partial Synchronization for DDP LLM Training
results.judge_reason: Layer-wise scheduled partial synchronization for distributed LLM training is the closest prior art to the proposed mast-pulse schedule and is cited in the novelty claim.

results.title: Partial Parameter Updates for Efficient Distributed Training
results.judge_reason: Partial-parameter-update scheme for distributed LLM training directly overlaps the proposed reduced-sync regime that the candidate must differentiate against.

results.title: Distributed training of LLMs: A survey
results.judge_reason: Survey of distributed LLM training (aggregation, async updates) provides general landscape against which the mast-pulse schedule would be evaluated.

results.title: Comprehensive Survey on Distributed Deep Learning Training (preprints 2025-12)
results.judge_reason: Broad survey of distributed deep-learning parallelism strategies is relevant context but does not address byzantine adversaries or pulse schedules specifically.

results.title: Scalable Synthesis of Distributed LLM Workloads via Symbolic Tensor Graphs
results.judge_reason: Symbolic-tensor-graph workload synthesis is tangential to scheduling/synchronization choice and does not address the candidate's proposed mast-pulse mechanism or threat model.

verdict: Search results substantively support both the source-domain (mast-year synchrony, Moran effect, predator satiation) and LLM-side prior art (DreamDDP, partial parameter updates, distributed training surveys). Six of eight results hit; novelty claim is plausibly bounded by the cited reduced-sync work, but the byzantine-adversary / predator-satiation framing remains under-supported by the retrieved corpus, leaving the analogy's distinctive contribution intact but unverified against an exact prior.
